steps.title: Distribute the monomial to each term
steps.content:
Apply the distributive property. The monomial multiplies each term in the binomial:
$$(x^{-9}y^{6}z^{5}) \cdot (x^{4}z^{5}) + (x^{-9}y^{6}z^{5}) \cdot (y^{8}z^{-7})$$

steps.title: Apply exponent rules to each product
steps.content:
When multiplying powers with the same base, add the exponents.

First product: $x^{-9}y^{6}z^{5} \cdot x^{4}z^{5}$
$$x^{-9+4}y^{6}z^{5+5} = x^{-5}y^{6}z^{10}$$
Second product: $x^{-9}y^{6}z^{5} \cdot y^{8}z^{-7}$
$$x^{-9}y^{6+8}z^{5+(-7)} = x^{-9}y^{14}z^{-2}$$
Combined:
$$x^{-5}y^{6}z^{10}+x^{-9}y^{14}z^{-2}$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
